subject: Reg CB Counter Taobao Weekly Report 202501030
deck_filename: Counter Taobao Weekly Update - Week ending 30 Oct’ 25.pdf

content.answer:
Hi Ian, Jianghong,

Please find the weekly Counter Taobao project update as of 30 October below.

[image: image.png]

Table: Counter TB Program Performance SEA + TW


[image: image.png]

Table: Counter TB Net Price Competitiveness Performance SEA + TW

Key Highlights


   -

   Overall:
   -

      Topline: Oct MTD Program ADO 131K, RR 92%, MoM +10%, ADG 1.23M, RR,
      88%, MoM +12%, ADO% to CB at 7.3%. underachieved mainly due to
VN, 40K ADO
      (83% RR), MoM +1% as a result of the introduction of higher custom fees
      which lead to less competitive assortment pricing. Next Steps:
1) Focus on
      shifting top program sellers to 3PF model, program top seller 岛屿 has
      agreed, while discussion with other sellers are ongoing and target to
      complete by Mid-Nov 2) Focus on seed shop campaign participation in Nov.
      -

      SKU#: SKU# at 2.3M (83% RR) due to lever 3 underachievement.


   -

   Fashion:
   -

      Topline: ADO 70.6K (RR 96%), ADG $627.5K (RR 93%), ADO% to CB 8.3%
      (MoM +0.9pp). Overall Fashion performance is on track except for Lever 2
      due to VN under performance. Lever 3 targets have been adjusted,
factoring
      in P&L considerations and is track (RR 102%)
      -

      SKU#: 1.76M (RR 81%), MoM +51%
      -

      Program PC2: -1.4% (MoM -1.5pp)
      -

   Furniture:
   -

      Topline: ADO 9.7K (RR 94%), ADG $262.8K (RR 78%), ADO% to CB 0.6%
      (MoM +0.0 pp) mainly due to SG underachievement as top seller
RZY(润泽优) are
      under penalty due to bad seller ops and reduced Ads spend. Next step: We
      have removed shops to focus on a list of more cooperative shops under RZY
      while we focus efforts to acquire better sellers to replace RZY.
      -

      SKU#: 340K (RR 82%), MoM -7%
      -

      Program PC2: 2.3% (MoM -2.6pp)
      -

   F&B
   -

      Topline: ADO 2.6K (RR 71%), ADG $15.4K (RR 66%), ADO% to CB L1 4.0%

content.question: Focus on
*Lever Performance Update:*
*[Net Price Comp Improvement]*

content.answer:
ERP/SHP Mkt pitching: This is the most successful source with 139
   sellers and a 65% success rate due to SHP Mkt team pitching sellers 1:1
   together with ERP with more credibility.

Shopee-led pitching: Total of 16 sellers with 1% success rate, which is
   lower than Lever 1’s success rate at ~2%. The gap is due to sellers not
   trusting Shopee’s calls/texts as they do not know who we are, and do not
   want to take the time to know.

Mass pitching: Total of 134 sellers success rate at 0.1%, which includes
   various pitching sources such as events / livestreams / PR articles / etc.



Can we go into more specifics into what is going well/not well to grow ADO
contribution more?


Noted. We are currently preparing a program review of the 3 growth drivers
and we target to do more moving forward. Will put more details in the
upcoming Reg CB projects monthly, a summary below:

*Shopee-led pitching:* Total of 16 sellers with 1% success rate,
*Mass pitching: *Total of 134 sellers success rate at 0.1%, which

*Lever 1:* A large number of uncontactable sellers, 60% of the top
*Lever 3:* Fewer seller pitches via ERPs (JST and WDT) than
*Lever 1:* Refresh top TB pool to continue acquisition and place more
*Lever 2:* Continue to SKU sourcing via existing sellers, while
*Lever 3*: Focus more resources into JST/WDT/OF to acquire more
*Net Price Comp (NPC) Effectiveness *
*Program Incentive Effectiveness*
*Others*
Improve KOL uptake of TB SKUs
Improve seller/SKU naming convention for better search
Do offline affiliate marketing (XHS etc)

content.answer:
ERP/SHP Mkt pitching: This channel has 85 sellers who listed with 2.2k
   ADO or ~50%
SAI pitching: This channel has 10 sellers who listed with 0.4k ADO or ~9%

Mass pitching: This channel has 52 sellers who listed with 1.9k ADO or
   or ~41%


What is the mass pitching blocker?


This refers to the initial expectation that ERPs will show a banner
advertising Shopee’s partnership on their main page which did not
materialise due to fear of offending other e-commerce platforms. Instead,
we pivoted to the 3 sources of acquisition that we shared in the table
above, with the mass pitching instead diverting to other sources such as
livestreams, PR articles and events.


We will not focus on OF. For JST and WDT, what else can we do there?


We target to onboard ~50 sellers moving forward each month, and will place
more focus on mass pitching via events/livestreams/PR articles instead as
it is the quickest to scale. Specifically in Nov, we target to get 33
sellers from mass pitching.


For the other 2 channels referring to ERP/SHP Mkt pitching and SAI
pitching, we will still target to bring in ~20 sellers per month but it is
harder to scale because

(1) ERP/SHP Mkt pitching: a) ERP and their sellers are quite focused on
peak season sales with limited resources on our program b) ERPs require our
orders to be larger to scale this program further.

(2) SAI pitching: is very low efficiency, our success rate is 1.1% vs Lever
1’s ~2% success. The gap is due to domestic TB sellers not knowing who
Shopee is. We suggested trying this later after rounds of PR articles in Q4.


[image: image.png]


What happened here? And how/when are we cleaning this up?


This issue is specific to existing sellers (Lever 2) only as the current
program incentive offers seller shop vouchers which apply to the full shop
assortment.



   -

   In SG, from Aug to Oct a total of 9% of shop voucher value set up
   ($57.6K) is spent on non-TB program SKU.
   -

   In MY, from Aug to Oct a total of 10% of shop voucher value set up
   ($127.3K) is spent on non-TB program SKU.

In terms of ADO, ERP (not counting OF) makes up 4.5k ADO or 80% of our

In terms of SKU#, ERP (not counting OF) makes up 0.65 mil or 25% of our

content.answer:
Hi Ian,

Please find our responses below:

I thought WDT, JST were introducing us to their key merchants so that we
could acquire 1 by 1? We only have contacts for ~200 sellers after so long?


For JST, the sellers provided are the top 10% on TB by GMV within their
respective categories. They gave us 221 leads, from which we acquired
sellers one by one. These sellers are generating approximately 4.4K ADO,
accounting for 80% of Swarm, with an SKU efficiency of around 1%. Moving
forward, JST will continue to provide us with 50 sellers per month, and we
are capped to this number.

For the JST one-by-one pitch, we prioritize the quality of sellers over
quantity. These sellers require more effort to pitch and onboard compared
to others, which is why we target 50 sellers per month (around 200 sellers
over the past four months). On average, each seller can bring us around
3,000 SKUs.

In contrast, WDT provided only 4 leads, as they are currently focused on
improving their internal systems in Q4. Without a fully integrated system,
the seller OPS UX is quite messy, so we decided to pause onboarding since
October. We plan to resume onboarding WDT sellers after their internal
system improvements, targeted for completion in Q1 Y26.



For Lever 2, who is governing the inclusion of SKUs right now? To indeed
verify that these are TB skus as compared to SLS sellers trying to pull a
fast one


The local CB team currently manages the process of setting up
Shopee-sponsored shop vouchers for sellers. To ensure that future shop
vouchers are applied only to TB SKUs, we will establish exclusion criteria
for other assortments in the shop that are not part of the program’s focus.

Best Regards,

Max Goh

Address: 1 Fusionopolis Place, #09-10, Galaxis, Singapore 138522

content.question:
Hi Ian,



Please find our responses below:



I thought WDT, JST were introducing us to their key merchants so that we
could acquire 1 by 1?
We only have contacts for ~200 sellers after so long?

For *JST*, the sellers provided are the top 10% on TB by GMV within their
respective categories. They gave us 221 leads, from which we acquired
sellers one by one. These sellers are generating approximately 4.4K ADO,
accounting for 80% of Swarm, with an SKU efficiency of around 1%. Moving
forward, JST will continue to provide us with 50 sellers per month, and we
are capped to this number.

For the JST one-by-one pitch, we prioritize the *quality* of sellers over
quantity. These sellers require more effort to pitch and onboard compared
to others, which is why we target 50 sellers per month (around 200 sellers
over the past four months). On average, each seller can bring us around
3,000 SKUs.

In contrast, *WDT* provided only 4 leads, as they are currently focused on
improving their internal systems in Q4. Without a fully integrated system,
the seller OPS UX is quite messy, so we decided to pause onboarding since
October. We plan to resume onboarding WDT sellers after their internal
system improvements, targeted for completion in Q1 Y26.

@Shuning Wang <shuning.wang@shopee.com> as discussed, please work with
@Jianghong
Liu <jianghong.liu@shopee.com> side on

   - Ensuring that we are managing the budget for Swamp in the right way
   - Need to see how we can accelerate more sellers

 For Lever 2, who is governing the inclusion of SKUs right now?

To indeed
verify that these are TB skus as compared to SLS sellers trying to pull a
fast one



The local CB team currently manages the process of setting up
Shopee-sponsored shop vouchers for sellers. To ensure that future shop
vouchers are applied only to TB SKUs, we will establish exclusion criteria
for other assortments in the shop that are not part of the program’s focus.

Yes, but who defines that these are TB SKUs?

content.answer:
Hi Ian,

Yes, but who defines that these are TB SKUs?

Reg CB Project team defines what TB SKUs are and governs the process. And
TB SKUs will be defined as

   -

   All SKUs that are uploaded by Lever 1 and 3 (New acquired sellers from
   TB seller wishlist or via JST/WDT/OF)
   -

   All wishlisted SKUs that are uploaded via Lever 2 (targeted sourcing by
   our existing SLS sellers)
   -

      For this, we work with BI team to run an automated script to ensure
      that they match with SKUs on the TB SKU wishlist
